title: Commercial Divers
description: Work below surface of water, using surface-supplied air or scuba equipment to inspect, repair, remove, or install equipment and structures. May use a variety of power and hand tools, such as drills, sledgehammers, torches, and welding equipment. May conduct tests or experiments, rig explosives, or photograph structures or marine life.

tasks.text: Take appropriate safety precautions, such as monitoring dive lengths and depths and registering with authorities before diving expeditions begin.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Check and maintain diving equipment, such as helmets, masks, air tanks, harnesses, or gauges.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Communicate with workers on the surface while underwater, using signal lines or telephones.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Descend into water with the aid of diver helpers, using scuba gear or diving suits.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Obtain information about diving tasks and environmental conditions.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Supervise or train other divers, including hobby divers.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Inspect the condition of underwater steel or wood structures.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Inspect and test docks, ships, buoyage systems, plant intakes or outflows, or underwater pipelines, cables, or sewers, using closed circuit television, still photography, and testing equipment.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Repair ships, bridge foundations, or other structures below the water line, using caulk, bolts, and hand tools.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Recover objects by placing rigging around sunken objects, hooking rigging to crane lines, and operating winches, derricks, or cranes to raise objects.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Operate underwater video, sonar, recording, or related equipment to investigate underwater structures or marine life.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Take test samples or photographs to assess the condition of vessels or structures.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Cut and weld steel, using underwater welding equipment, jigs, and supports.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Install, inspect, clean, or repair piping or valves.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Carry out non-destructive testing, such as tests for cracks on the legs of oil rigs at sea.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Install pilings or footings for piers or bridges.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Salvage wrecked ships or their cargo, using pneumatic power velocity and hydraulic tools and explosive charges, when necessary.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Remove obstructions from strainers or marine railway or launching ways, using pneumatic or power hand tools.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Set or guide placement of pilings or sandbags to provide support for structures, such as docks, bridges, cofferdams, or platforms.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Perform activities related to underwater search and rescue, salvage, recovery, or cleanup operations.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Perform offshore oil or gas exploration or extraction duties, such as conducting underwater surveys or repairing and maintaining drilling rigs or platforms.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Drill holes in rock and rig explosives for underwater demolitions.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Remove rubbish or pollution from the sea.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
